hypothesis: PCI is inflated because it rewards compliance (field presence, classifiability) without measuring whether falsifications lead to actual learning (lesson updates)
method: Add epistemic_yield component to orient_pci.py: scan recent falsified experiments, check if corresponding frontiers have lesson follow-ups in same session window

expected.pci_impact: PCI drops 5-15% from adding yield factor

actual.gap: F-EVAL2 falsified with no recent lesson follow-up

verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — PCI was inflated (confirmed), but epistemic yield is higher than expected (94% vs predicted 60-80%). The larger PCI drop (-17.1%) comes from combined effect of L-1526 classifiability fix + yield factor, not yield alone
steerer_signal: skeptical-empiricist S528: PCI measures field presence not prediction accuracy
